session.deleteDescription: “{title}” will be permanently removed along with its messages. This can't be undone.

profile.title: Your profile.
profile.intro: Edit the name the axolotl calls you, the place it reports in the terminal footer, and how times and temperatures are displayed.
profile.emailHelp: Not editable yet — will require verification.
profile.localityHelp: Shown in the terminal footer as ● LOCAL · locality.
profile.timeFormatHelp: Applies to the terminal footer clock.
profile.temperatureHelp: Applies to the terminal footer weather.

home.title: Your axolotl is listening.
greetings: Bubbles are up. What shall we explore?
I've been practising my reasoning while you were away.
home.empty: No conversations yet — start one from the hero above.

relativeTime.minutes: {count, plural, one {# min ago} other {# min ago}}
relativeTime.hours: {count, plural, one {# h ago} other {# h ago}}
relativeTime.days: {count, plural, one {# d ago} other {# d ago}}

auth.loginIntro: Sign in to chat with your axolotl.

drawer.title: Chat controls
drawer.modelHelp: Blank = whatever vLLM is serving. Override per session if you point at a second model.
drawer.samplingHelp: Overrides apply to this session only.

temperature.label: Temperature
temperature.description: Higher = more creative / random. 0 is deterministic.

top_p.label: Top-p
top_p.description: Nucleus sampling cutoff. Keeps the smallest set of tokens whose probability sums to this.

top_k.label: Top-k
top_k.description: Consider only the k most likely tokens each step.

min_p.label: Min-p
min_p.description: Drop tokens whose probability is below this fraction of the top token.

presence_penalty.label: Presence penalty
presence_penalty.description: Positive values push the model toward new topics rather than repeating.

repetition_penalty.label: Repetition penalty
repetition_penalty.description: Scales down tokens that have already appeared. 1.0 disables.

max_tokens.label: Max tokens
max_tokens.description: Cap the length of the model's reply.

personas.title: Your personas.
personas.intro: Named system prompts the axolotl can take on — attach one when starting a conversation, or edit the wording here.
personas.empty: No personas yet — create one to shape how the axolotl responds.

modal.name: Name
modal.systemPromptHelp: Applied as the system message when a session uses this persona.
modal.promptPlaceholder: You are a helpful axolotl companion…

delete.title: Delete *persona*?
delete.description: “{name}” will be removed. Sessions already using it keep their stored prompt; new sessions can no longer select it.

sandbox.title: Design sandbox.
sandbox.intro: Every primitive in the DA — typography, colours, shadows, buttons, pills, inputs, modals, palette, chat bubbles, mascot. If a new pattern doesn't land cleanly here, it isn't ready to ship.

tools.title: Pick what the axolotl can reach for.
tools.intro: Each tool is local-first. Toggle any off and the model will stop calling it.
tools.syncFailed: Last sync failed — see MCP settings.

web_search.title: Web search
web_search.description: Search the web via DuckDuckGo. Use ONLY when the information needed is recent, specific, or clearly outside your training data. Returns up to N results with URL, title and snippet.

model.title: Model & generation.
model.intro: Your baseline sampling parameters. Any value you don't set falls back to the server default. Every new session starts from these, and you can override them per-conversation from the chat drawer (<kbd>⌘,</kbd>).

reasoning.title: Reasoning phase.
reasoning.intro: Controls the model's think block — the internal monologue it writes before committing to an answer. You can still override this per-session from the chat drawer.
reasoning.explainerBody: Models trained with chain-of-thought emit a hidden-style monologue first — the UI shows it in a collapsed block above the reply. Turning it off tends to produce terser, more direct answers but hurts multi-step reasoning.

options.onSub: Always emit the “think” phase before the answer.
options.offSub: Skip the inner monologue. Faster, but shallower on hard questions.
options.defaultSubOn: Whatever the backend is configured for (currently on).
options.defaultSubOff: Whatever the backend is configured for (currently off).

mcp.title: MCP servers.
mcp.intro: Connect Model Context Protocol servers to plug their tools into the chat. Sync to fetch the tool list; toggle individual tools on the <link>Tools</link> page.
mcp.empty: No MCP servers yet. Add one to start connecting external tools.
mcp.syncedTools: {count, plural, =0 {no tools} one {# tool} other {# tools}}

modal.name: Name
modal.nameHelp: Letters, digits, dashes, underscores. Used to namespace the tools.
modal.urlHelp: Streamable-HTTP MCP endpoint. SSE-only servers aren't supported in this iteration.
modal.tokenHelp: Stored encrypted at rest (Fernet). Sent as `Authorization: Bearer …` on every call.

delete.title: Remove *MCP server*?
delete.description: “{name}” will be unregistered. Its tools stop appearing in the registry. Past tool calls in conversations are preserved.

toasts.syncedCount: {count, plural, =0 {no tools synced} one {# tool synced} other {# tools synced}}
